title: A ribbon ZX calculus for gauge theory
authors: Gabriel Wong, Razin A. Shaikh, William Donnelly
abstract: ZX calculus provides a graphical formalism for reasoning about quantum processes, built from two interacting Frobenius algebras associated with the Z and X bases of a qubit. While it has found widespread application in quantum information and computing, its relationship to quantum field theory has only recently begun to be explored. In this work, we further develop this connection by providing a generalization of ZX calculus to two-dimensional Yang Mills theory with a compact gauge group. The key observation is that both frameworks can be organized around the Hopf Frobenius algebraic structure associated with a group algebra, which can in turn be described by the diagrammatics of two dimensional topological quantum field theory. Given the well known relationship between gauge theory and gravity in two and three dimensions, our work paves the way for applications of ZX to low dimensional gravity.
summary: This paper develops a 'ribbon ZX calculus' to connect the graphical rules of ZX calculus to two-dimensional Yang-Mills theory. By showing that both systems share an underlying Hopf-Frobenius algebraic structure, it provides a topological framework for calculating gauge theories on curved spaces. This work is significant for applying diagrammatic quantum methods to low-dimensional gravity and string theory.
main_problem: The work aims to establish a deep mathematical connection between ZX-calculus, a graphical tool for quantum processes, and Quantum Field Theory (QFT), specifically two-dimensional Yang-Mills (2DYM) theory.
main_result: A 'ribbon ZX calculus' is developed that maps the structure of 2D TQFT diagrams onto topological equivalences within the ZX framework, providing a continuum generalization for gauge theories.
method: The connection is formalized by showing that both frameworks are governed by Hopf-Frobenius algebraic structures associated with group algebras ($L^2(G)$).
model_or_system: The primary model is 2D Yang-Mills theory on manifolds with boundaries, whose open sector dynamics are described by an open Frobenius algebra. The mathematical machinery involves mapping TQFT cobordisms to ribbon graphs.
key_observables: The partition function exhibits area dependence governed by the quadratic Casimir $C_2(R)$; observables relate to holonomies and anyon fusion rules.
important_parameters: Gauge coupling constant ($g$), manifold area ($A$), and the structure of the compact gauge group ($G$).
main_assumptions: The formalism relies on interpreting ribbon graphs as entangled anyon worldlines obeying modular tensor category rules, though defining the evaluation map for infinite groups is noted as a technical challenge.
figures_summary: Not specified in the notes; the structure suggests diagrams illustrating the mapping between TQFT cobordisms/ribbon graphs and ZX-calculus elements (spiders, etc.).
paper_structure: The paper builds from introducing the general connection via Frobenius algebras to developing the 'ribbon' formalism for 2DYM on manifolds with boundaries. It details how open and closed sectors relate using concepts like 'zippers,' confirms the Hopf algebra structure via the antipode, and discusses generalizations to $q$-deformed theories and gravity.
why_interesting: It provides a powerful diagrammatic language (ZX calculus) to analyze fundamental QFT structures like gauge theory and topological order, bridging quantum computation formalism with high-energy physics concepts.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
